bensalem2015.author: Ben Salem, M., Affes, H., Ksouda, K., Dhouibi, R., Sahnoun, Z., Hammami, S. and Zeghal, K.M.
bensalem2015.title: Pharmacological Studies of Artichoke Leaf Extract and Their Health Benefits

ema.author: European Medicines Agency (HMPC)
ema.title: European Union herbal monograph on Cynara cardunculus L. (syn. Cynara scolymus L.), folium (Cynarae folium)

actions.action: Digestive aid for dyspeptic complaints - indigestion, bloating and fullness
Choleretic - stimulates bile flow to aid fat digestion
Hepatoprotective / liver support (protects liver cells and may aid their regeneration)

constituents.name: Caffeoylquinic acids (chlorogenic acid and cynarin)
constituents.note: The principal choleretic, hepatoprotective and antioxidant constituents.

constituents.name: Sesquiterpene lactones (cynaropicrin)
constituents.note: The bitter principle that stimulates digestion.

contraindications.note: Because it stimulates bile flow, artichoke is contraindicated in bile-duct obstruction and should only be used under medical supervision if you have gallstones.
As a member of the daisy family (Asteraceae), it can cause allergic reactions in sensitive people; use in pregnancy and breastfeeding is not recommended.

internal_notes: Enriched 2026-06-12 from scientific and regulatory literature (Plant Foods for Human Nutrition pharmacological review; EMA HMPC monograph). Originally a skeleton imported from the omniasana.bio Symptom-to-Plant Lookup tool (indigestion, bloating, liver-support, high-cholesterol, ibs); associations retained and now part-mapped and cited.

hellinger2013.author: Hellinger, R., Koehbach, J., Fedchuk, H., Sauer, B., Huber, R., Gruber, C.W. and Grundemann, C.
hellinger2013.title: Immunosuppressive activity of an aqueous Viola tricolor herbal extract

actions.action: Anti-inflammatory / immunomodulatory for chronic skin conditions - eczema, psoriasis, itching and scabs (traditional, supported by cyclotide research)
Traditional demulcent / expectorant for cough, bronchitis and chest inflammation

constituents.name: Cyclotides (cyclic plant peptides)
constituents.note: Bioactive peptides shown to suppress over-active immune (T-cell) responses - the rationale for use in inflammatory skin disease.

contraindications.note: Regarded as safe in customary traditional use; because the extract suppresses immune-cell activity, there is a theoretical caution when taking immunosuppressant medicines.
Contains salicylates, so use caution if you are salicylate-sensitive; safety in pregnancy and breastfeeding is not established.

internal_notes: Enriched 2026-06-12 from scientific literature (Journal of Ethnopharmacology study identifying immunosuppressive cyclotides). Originally a skeleton imported from the omniasana.bio Symptom-to-Plant Lookup tool (eczema, skin-irritation, cough); associations retained and now part-mapped and cited.

ellis2022.author: Ellis, L.R., Zulfiqar, S., Holmes, M., Marshall, L., Dye, L. and Boesch, C.
ellis2022.title: A systematic review and meta-analysis of the effects of Hibiscus sabdariffa on blood pressure and cardiometabolic markers

garciamunoz2023.author: Garcia-Munoz, A.M., Garcia-Guillen, A.I., Victoria-Montesinos, D., Abellan-Ruiz, M.S., Alburquerque-Gonzalez, B. and Canovas, F.
garciamunoz2023.title: Effect of the Combination of Hibiscus sabdariffa with Other Plant Extracts in the Prevention of Metabolic Syndrome: A Systematic Review and Meta-Analysis

actions.action: Lowers blood pressure, with the greatest effect in people with raised blood pressure
Improves the lipid profile - lowers LDL and total cholesterol

constituents.name: Anthocyanins (delphinidin and cyanidin glucosides)
constituents.note: The red pigments and principal antioxidant/vasoactive constituents.

constituents.name: Organic acids (including hibiscus/hydroxycitric acid) and polyphenols
constituents.note: Contribute to the tartness and cardiometabolic effects.

contraindications.note: Its blood-pressure-lowering effect can add to that of antihypertensive medicines; monitor blood pressure if you take them.
May alter the absorption or levels of some medicines (e.g. paracetamol, chloroquine); avoid concentrated or medicinal doses in pregnancy.

internal_notes: Enriched 2026-06-12 from scientific literature (Nutrition Reviews meta-analysis; Foods systematic review/meta-analysis). Originally a skeleton imported from the omniasana.bio Symptom-to-Plant Lookup tool (high-blood-pressure, high-cholesterol); associations retained and now part-mapped and cited.

jamshidi2017.author: Jamshidi, N. and Cohen, M.M.
jamshidi2017.title: The Clinical Efficacy and Safety of Tulsi in Humans: A Systematic Review of the Literature

bhattacharyya2013.author: Bhattacharyya, P. and Bishayee, A.
bhattacharyya2013.title: Ocimum sanctum Linn. (Tulsi): an ethnomedicinal plant for the prevention and treatment of cancer

actions.action: Adaptogen for stress and anxiety (improves stress scores and well-being in trials)
Supports blood-sugar control (lowers fasting and post-meal glucose)

constituents.name: Volatile oil rich in eugenol
constituents.note: The principal aromatic constituent, with anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activity.

constituents.name: Ursolic acid, rosmarinic acid, apigenin and luteolin

contraindications.note: Generally well tolerated; mild nausea has been reported. Its blood-sugar-lowering effect means caution alongside antidiabetic medicines.
Animal studies suggest mild anti-fertility and antiplatelet effects, so avoid medicinal doses when pregnant or trying to conceive, and before surgery.

internal_notes: Enriched 2026-06-12 from scientific literature (Evidence-Based Complementary and Alternative Medicine systematic review of clinical efficacy; Anti-Cancer Drugs ethnomedicinal review). Ocimum tenuiflorum = Ocimum sanctum. Originally a skeleton (merged tool + Materia Medica stub) from omniasana.bio (stress, anxiety, blood-sugar, immune-support); now fully part-mapped and cited.

ema.author: European Medicines Agency (HMPC)
ema.title: European Union herbal monograph on Marrubium vulgare L., herba (Marrubii herba)

actions.action: Expectorant for productive cough and catarrh of the upper respiratory tract / bronchitis
Bitter digestive for dyspeptic complaints (indigestion, bloating, flatulence) and loss of appetite

constituents.name: Labdane diterpene marrubiin
constituents.note: The characteristic bitter principle (pharmacopoeial material contains at least 0.7%); drives the expectorant and digestive actions.

constituents.name: Tannins and volatile oil
constituents.note: Astringent and aromatic constituents of the herb.

contraindications.note: As a strong bitter it may aggravate hyperacidity; theoretical caution in peptic ulcer. Large amounts have shown effects on heart rhythm and blood pressure in studies, so avoid excessive intake.
Traditional use only; a cough or digestive complaint that persists needs medical assessment. Use in pregnancy and breastfeeding is not recommended.

internal_notes: Enriched 2026-06-12 from regulatory and phytotherapy literature (EMA HMPC monograph for Marrubii herba; ESCOP monograph). Originally a skeleton imported from the omniasana.bio Symptom-to-Plant Lookup tool (cough, sore-throat, indigestion, bloating); associations retained and now part-mapped and cited.
